questions.question:
Consider all the evidence you gathered about how setting influences Brian and Jiang. In one sentence, write a claim that compares and contrasts the role that setting plays in influencing the characters and events in both texts. 

Your claim should address both similarities and differences in how the wilderness setting in “Hatchet” and the school setting in “Red Scarf Girl” affect the characters.

responses.content:
The setting affects Jiang because her whole family is getting exposed in front of all her teachers, classmates, and friends, by the people who are interrogating her.

Brian’s setting affects him because he has no food, no water, and no fire so he has to face all of those situations by himself.

The setting affects Jiang because her whole family is getting exposed in front of all her teachers, classmates, and friends, by the people who are interrogating her.

Brian’s setting affects him because he has no food, no water, and no fire so he has to face all of those situations by himself.

Brian’s setting affects him because he has no food, no water, and no fire so he has to face all of those situations by himself.

questions.question:
“Red Scarf Girl” and “Hatchet” feature young people trapped in challenging situations. In both texts, the setting provides the context for the main conflict or problem. Compare and contrast the role that the setting plays in influencing the characters and events of the two texts. 

Respond to this question in the form of a paragraph that includes a claim, evidence from both texts, and explanation.

responses.content:
The setting influences the characters from both of the texts in a certain way listed down below

In Hatch it Brian is affected by his settings because he has no food, no water, and no fireplace. He also doesn’t really have anything to help him make a helpful trap to catch small animals for a source of food.  His mind is thinking that he is going to be recused in the spawn of a day or two just like in the movies he saw but in reality it’s going to probably take longer since he is stranded in a forest.

The school setting affects Jiang because her whole family is being exposed by people she doesn’t even know. She is being exposed in front of her friends and teachers, and classmates. She thinks this will affect her in her learning and social experiences.  She is also nervous and doesn’t know what to do since the people that are asking questions about her father are trying to force her to turn against her father.

responses.bucket_rationale: The response identifies specific dangers from the text: surviving on top of a volcano, lava spitting out, burns on Donna's face, and sharp rocks in her hair. responses.content: She’s trying to convey that the media can be good sometimes and be useful since the media helped her.
